evals.name: csv-export-feature
evals.prompt: We need to add CSV export to our reporting dashboard. Users should be able to click a button on the /reports page and download the current filtered view as a CSV. The dataset can be large (100k+ rows) so it can't block the request. Break this into implementation tasks.
evals.expected_output: A dependency-ordered series of separate, well-scoped engineering tasks (each with title, context, testable acceptance criteria, technical guidance as pointers, out-of-scope, dependencies, and definition-of-done/testing notes). Should account for async/non-blocking generation of a large export.
assertions: Output is a series of >=3 separate task files (TASK-NNN.md), not one combined doc
Every task contains all seven required fields (Context/Background, Acceptance Criteria, Technical Guidance, Out of Scope, Dependencies, Definition of Done/Testing Notes)
Every task title is imperative/action-oriented (leads with a verb, names a concrete target)
Acceptance criteria are testable (checklist or Given/When/Then), not vague goals
Dependencies point backward only (no task depends on a higher-numbered task)
Out of Scope is explicitly stated

evals.name: auth-rate-limiting-hardening
evals.prompt: Our /api/auth/login and /api/auth/reset-password endpoints have no rate limiting and we're getting hit with credential-stuffing attacks. I want this fixed properly - break the work into tasks I can hand to the team.
evals.expected_output: A series of separate, dependency-ordered tasks that harden the auth endpoints, each with the seven required fields, testable acceptance criteria, and explicit out-of-scope. Should treat this as security hardening (e.g. limiter, response behavior, observability) rather than one monolithic task.
assertions: Output is a series of >=3 separate task files (TASK-NNN.md), not one combined doc
Every task contains all seven required fields (Context/Background, Acceptance Criteria, Technical Guidance, Out of Scope, Dependencies, Definition of Done/Testing Notes)
Every task title is imperative/action-oriented (leads with a verb, names a concrete target)
Acceptance criteria are testable (checklist or Given/When/Then), not vague goals
Dependencies point backward only (no task depends on a higher-numbered task)
Out of Scope is explicitly stated

evals.name: payment-service-refactor
evals.prompt: We have payment logic copy-pasted across three controllers (CheckoutController, SubscriptionController, RefundController). I want to extract it into a single PaymentService without changing behavior. Plan this out as a series of tasks.
evals.expected_output: A series of separate, dependency-ordered, behavior-preserving refactor tasks, each with the seven required fields and testable acceptance criteria. Should emphasize no behavior change, incremental/mergeable steps, and characterization tests before extraction.
assertions: Output is a series of >=3 separate task files (TASK-NNN.md), not one combined doc
Every task contains all seven required fields (Context/Background, Acceptance Criteria, Technical Guidance, Out of Scope, Dependencies, Definition of Done/Testing Notes)
Every task title is imperative/action-oriented (leads with a verb, names a concrete target)
Acceptance criteria are testable (checklist or Given/When/Then), not vague goals
Dependencies point backward only (no task depends on a higher-numbered task)
Out of Scope is explicitly stated
